action.reasoning: Observation: best 0.594 from iter16 (replay=500/task balanced post-task via meta+torch.where/randperm, KD beta=0.5/T=2.0 with proper KLDiv(log_softmax(new/T)||softmax(old/T))*T**2, consol=5@1e-4 on mem, Adam(5e-4/1e-5), per-task Cosine reset, dense logs+val/best per epoch, deepcopy old_model). Recent iters 17-25 all ~0.08-0.58 (last 0.1704) due to persistent train.py truncation (ends after images.to(device) in loop; missing opt.step/scheduler/KD/full epochs/mem_update_after_task/consol/best-tracking; late tasks random ~0.04 acc, val~0.07). Curves show no post-task0 learning. Analysis: long train.py triggers workspace truncation; successful iter16 had COMPLETE code with all components working (val~0.596 matching test, balanced task accs early~0.48/late~0.7-0.8). Root cause fixed by concise full rewrite. Decision: provide COMPLETE concise non-truncated train.py exactly replicating iter16 success params (update mem AFTER task train only, full KD only if task>0, per-epoch val+best on val.pt, final best ckpt with model_state_dict). Update predict.py for robust loading (handles state_dict or full ckpt, correct transforms/inference on test images). Run train then submit_eval on checkpoint.pt to recover >=0.594 (target >0.60).
